title: `podman image scp` could skip cached layers
body:
### Feature request description

Podman image scp works great for minimal deployment scenarios without a registry. However it could act smarter pushing an image by skipping cached layers similar to pushing and pulling from a registry.

### Suggest potential solution

image scp could use a new option flag to opt in to registry-like behavior.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Using a registry would work but requires additional setup and management of permissions.

comments.body: The point of scp is to be a simple wrapper for podman save | podman load basically. Adding more complexity on top is IMO undesirable and not something I would like.

@Luap99 thanks for the quick answer.

I understand that this feature is opposed to "Keep it simple" for podman, but as far as i understand, the next best way to transfer an image between two machines is to go through a registry which makes it a two-step process and requires to have such a registry exposed publicly and access control managed for that registry too.

Scp really simplified the process of transfering an image to just one target machine for me, but when there is only a small change transfering the whole image around the globe feels quite inefficient.

Having a "smarter" scp function would be a big step to "keep it simple" on the user side while offering even faster "pushes" than using a registry.

it would be really cool if this were the case or skopeo could do this.  but i think @Luap99 is tugging on a good point. your idea is to be more efficient where we have cited simple.  registries are still the most efficient way to do this.  but given this is a feature request, maybe someone will want to decide to do this.  ofc, pull requests are welcome though i'd recommend verifying the approach with the team before creating a big PR.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
